source.description: Six-panel built-in image generation contact sheet saved under drafts/watermelon-wedge-doodle/ and cropped into raster tutorial assets.
source.notes: Backdated archive lesson kept honest in public copy; summer subject chosen for a bold marker shape distinct from existing flames, pizza, trophy, firework, and robot lessons.

process_strategy: A single six-panel marker contact sheet keeps one watermelon wedge in the same angle while the process adds wedge, rind bands, face, seeds, color fills, and final marker cleanup.

final_elements.name: watermelon wedge shape
final_elements.description: A wide triangular slice with a curved bottom edge.

final_elements.name: green rind bands
final_elements.description: Two curved rind bands following the bottom edge of the wedge.

final_elements.name: tiny smiling face
final_elements.description: A small face with dot eyes and a curved smile on the fruit.

final_elements.name: black seed drops
final_elements.description: Small teardrop seed shapes spaced around the face.

final_elements.name: red and green marker fills
final_elements.description: Red fruit fill and green rind color with visible marker streaks.

frames.visible_job: Blocks in the wide triangular watermelon slice and curved bottom edge.
Adds two curved rind bands along the bottom edge.
Places the dot eyes and curved smile on the fruit area.
Adds small teardrop seed shapes around the face.
Adds red fruit fill and green rind fill with marker texture.

final_step.visible_job: Only thickens the established black outlines, strengthens the red and green marker fills, and clarifies small highlights already present.
allowed_changes: small highlight cleanup on established elements

rejection_checks: No major contour, prop, marking, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
